sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 14.43 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.14)

statement.latex: Suppose that a finite group$G$has the form$G=AB$, where$A$and$B$are nilpotent subgroups of classes$$and$$respectively; then$G$is soluble (O.\,H.\,Kegel, H.\,Wielandt, 1961). Although the derived length${ dl}(G)$of$G$need not be bounded by$+$(see Archive, 5.17), can one bound${ dl}(G)$by a (linear) function of$$and$$?

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
